purpose: Fixes a use-after-free issue in Bluetooth hci_sync by checking HCI_UNREGISTER flag before queuing commands.
Removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c since it's already included via linux/tty.h. This simplifies the code and reduces redundancy.
Updates documentation for the 'N/A' store metric in perf reports.
Fixes vma lock handling issues during splitting and unmapping in hugetlb, addressing race conditions and improper resource management.
The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies in the device tree bindings for the rt5682s codec.
Fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one() by adding missing iounmap(), preventing memory issues.
The commit removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx to clean up the code.
The commit changes the key_idx field in cfg80211_auth_request from unsigned to signed to handle -1 correctly and avoid static checker warnings.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs without functional changes.
The commit introduces a helper function to check if the chip's FIFOs are in CAN-FD mode, replacing an open-coded condition for better maintainability.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip when RTC hardware is not present, improving test reliability by avoiding unnecessary failures.
Removes redundant variable assignment to fix a clang warning, improving code quality.
The commit modifies how rcuscale and RCU Tasks are built in the kernel to prevent unnecessary inclusion of RCU Tasks when rcuscale is present. This reduces kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text in a comment within spte.h, improving code readability.
Reverts a change that caused issues with the clk table, aiming to prevent underflow.
Adds a compatible string in device tree bindings to support the Mali-G31 GPU in the RZ/V2L SoC.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency in naming conventions within the DW PCIe core driver.
Fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier to match the promoted integer type.
Fixes a compatibility issue in MIPS jump label handling by casting an upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing negative offset warnings.
The commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() by directly calling the relevant function. This refactoring effort aims to eliminate redundant code and reduce the hal layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in arm64 code.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings since the provider only uses a single cell.
Allocates arrays in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for future driver changes allowing array size adjustments.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs in the socinfo driver to recognize new hardware platforms.
The commit updates the wifi driver rtl8xxxu to recognize all chip versions (A-P) instead of showing 'unknown' for versions beyond E. This prevents potential issues with unrecognized chips.
Removes redundant .is_enabled state in clock management to avoid duplication since the core already tracks it.
The commit aims to refactor code by removing #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usages, grouping similar cases together for cleaner code.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for future I2C node additions.
The commit fixes comments in the thermal driver for rzg2l by improving clarity and correcting typos.
Fixes an infinite loop caused by failing to acquire a HW semaphore during resource release when hardware is removed.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology for clocking, replacing old-style defines.
The commit removes unnecessary code related to IS_SWAPFILE in NFS, as it's always defined.
Implements GTP echo response handling to ensure compliance with GTP specifications by sending appropriate responses to Echo Requests.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate and introduces cpumask_first_zero(). It aims to improve function effectiveness.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver because the firmware no longer supports it, preventing an error message upon roaming.
Adds support for Intel platform with alc5682 codec without speaker amplifier by creating a new board config.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail when unsupported by the socket, preventing potential issues from such operations.
The commit modifies the kernel documentation build process to check for the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing xelatex errors on certain distributions where the package is missing.
Modifies checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros in switch statements, preventing false errors for existing kernel code.
Refactors simpledrm to use atomic helpers for better architecture and code sharing, no functional changes.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel operation of three hardware units on MT8195.
The commit removes VPRINTK() calls in libata as their information is redundant with existing tracepoints and also removes the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function for similar reasons.
Adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in FREESYNC mode to optimize power or performance.
Fixes a type mismatch in function parameter to resolve Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure.
Corrects a typo in device tree bindings by changing 'reset' to 'resets' and adds validation to prevent future issues.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adjusting the warning level. It does not fix the underlying bug but prevents future issues from being overlooked.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the crypto driver for sun8i-ss to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal within the io_uring subsystem, moving it into the filetable section. It also modifies helper functions to use ctx instead of io_kiocb for better context handling.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes for better separation under the vpu-blk-ctrl power domain.
The commit removes an unused macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) and integrates its content into another macro to simplify the code.
